title: Music: An Ode by Algernon Charles Swinburne — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
WAS it light that spake from the darkness,
or music that shone from the word,
When the night was enkindled with sound
of the sun or the first-born bird?
Souls enthralled and entrammelled in bondage
of seasons that fall and rise,
Bound fast round with the fetters of flesh,
and blinded with light that dies,
meaning: "Music: An Ode" by Algernon Charles Swinburne is a sad and deep and nature and inspirational poem consisting of 31 lines. The poet explores themes of sad and deep and nature and inspirational through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "WAS it light that spake from the darkness, or music that shone from the word,...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Algernon Charles Swinburne's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
